newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Only a routine Norges Bank 13F stake disclosure (June 6 syndicated article) and generic technical commentary. Today's +2.58% move looks flow/sector-driven, not fundamental; the last real data point remains the late-April Q1 2026 beat (EPS $1.18 vs $0.89 est) and raised FY26 guidance (sales $2.57-2.66B, adj EPS $4.80-5.05), which was already in the price and in yesterday's hold verdict.

sources.label: Markets Daily - Norges Bank takes position in Federal Signal (Jun 6, 2026)
Seeking Alpha - Federal Signal raises 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to $4.80-$5.05
MarketBeat - Federal Signal (FSS) news and analysis

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6 tying Federal Signal's safety/warning equipment to AI campuses or autonomous sites, and a +2.6% no-news day with YTD still slightly negative does not change the latent-node setup; yesterday's hold stands.
verdicts.bull: Q1 beat and raised FY26 guidance (18-22% sales growth) show a healthy core business; YTD -1.6% means the optional AI-campus/critical-infrastructure exposure is effectively free, preserving asymmetry if data-center safety/permitting spend materializes.
verdicts.neutral: Solid mid-cap industrial executing well, but the AI linkage remains conceptual - no disclosed data-center or AI-campus contracts - so it stays a deep latent node rather than a confirmed bottleneck name.
verdicts.bear: The AI thesis is a stretch: emergency vehicles and street sweepers have no demonstrated AI-capex pull-through, backlog fell 6% y/y, and at ~22x forward EPS the stock already prices in the industrial growth without any AI optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A disclosed contract or design win serving data-center campuses, utility/grid buildout, or autonomous-site safety systems; next checkpoint is Q2 earnings on July 29, 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI linkage never materializes and the name trades purely as a municipal/industrial cyclical, dead weight on an AI-nodes list if orders or margins soften.
